topic: GLM-5 and GLM-5.1 Review: Zhipu AI's Frontier Models for Developers

key_points: GLM-5.1 is Z.ai's post-training upgrade to GLM-5 base, released April 7, 2026
Primary differentiator: 8-hour sustained autonomous execution with 655 iterations (Linux desktop from scratch demo)
Experiment-analyze-optimize loop for long-horizon engineering tasks
58.4% on SWE-Bench Pro — first open-source model to top that leaderboard
Requires 8x H200 GPUs (860 GB VRAM) for BF16 local deployment
GLM-5.1 achieved SOTA on SWE-Bench Pro at time of release, beating GPT-5.4 (57.7%) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3%)
MIT license — fully open-weight, commercial use allowed, download and fine-tune freely
Text-only limitation: no multimodal input, unlike Claude Opus 4.6 which accepts images
Speed bottleneck: ~44.3 tokens/sec, slowest model in its competitive tier
GLM Coding Plan subscription at $3-30/month vs Claude Max at $100-200/month
GLM-5 released February 11-13, 2026 — 744B parameter MoE trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips
100,000 Huawei Ascend 910B chips used with MindSpore framework — full US semiconductor independence
API pricing: $0.60/M input, $1.92/M output — ~10x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.6
203K token context window; 128K max output tokens
50.4% on Humanity's Last Exam (beats Claude Opus 4.5), industry's lowest hallucination rate claimed
GLM-5.1 benchmarks: 58.4% SWE-Bench Pro, 42.7% NL2Repo, 63.5% Terminal-Bench 2.0, 68.7% CyberGym, 68.0% BrowseComp
Designed around sustained execution: handles ambiguous tasks with better judgment over extended sessions
vLLM and SGLang both supported — OpenAI-compatible API endpoint at localhost:8000/v1
Claude Opus 4.7 surpassed GLM-5.1 at 64.3% SWE-bench Pro in mid-April 2026
GLM-5.1 scores 45.3/100 in coding evaluation vs Claude Opus 4.6 at 47.9 (94.6% parity)
Math reasoning gap: AIME 2026 — GLM-5.1 95.3% vs GPT-5.4 98.7% and Claude Opus 4.6 98.2%
GPQA-Diamond shows even wider gap — weaker for research and quantitative tasks
Benchmark gaming concern: SWE-Rebench (decontaminated) shows more modest but still solid results
GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus edge ahead on complex multi-step tool-use workflows

stats.fact: GLM-5 has 744-745 billion total parameters in a MoE architecture with 256 experts, 8 activated per token (44B active per inference)
GLM-5 was trained on 28.5 trillion tokens using 100,000 Huawei Ascend 910B chips with MindSpore framework — no NVIDIA GPUs
GLM-5.1 achieved 58.4% on SWE-Bench Pro at launch (April 7, 2026), surpassing GPT-5.4 (57.7%) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3%) — first open-source model to top that leaderboard
GLM-5.1 demonstrated building a complete Linux desktop system from scratch within 8 hours, performing 655 autonomous iterations
GLM-5 API pricing: $0.60/M input, $1.92/M output — approximately 10x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.6
GLM-5.1 API pricing: $1.05/M input, $3.50/M output via OpenRouter
GLM Coding Plan subscription: $3-30/month vs Claude Max at $100-200/month
GLM-5.1 generates at approximately 44.3 tokens per second, slowest model in its competitive tier
GLM-5.1 scored 95.3% on AIME 2026 vs GPT-5.4 at 98.7% and Claude Opus 4.6 at 98.2%
Zhipu AI raised ~4.35 billion HKD ($558M USD) via Hong Kong IPO on January 8, 2026, then rebranded to Z.ai
GLM-5 in BF16 requires approximately 860 GB of VRAM — minimum 8x NVIDIA H200 GPUs for local deployment
GLM-5.1 scored 45.3 in coding evaluation (Claude Code as tester) vs Claude Opus 4.6 at 47.9 — 94.6% parity at ~10x lower cost
GLM-5.1 additional benchmarks: 42.7% NL2Repo, 63.5% Terminal-Bench 2.0, 68.7% CyberGym, 68.0% BrowseComp
Claude Opus 4.7 surpassed GLM-5.1 on SWE-bench Pro in mid-April 2026, reaching 64.3% vs GLM-5.1's 58.4%

angles: Geopolitical angle: GLM-5 as first frontier model trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips — what this means in the US export control era
Budget alternative: GLM-5 at $0.60/M tokens vs Claude Opus at $15/M — when is 94.6% parity good enough?
The 8-hour autonomous agent: GLM-5.1's long-horizon execution and experiment-analyze-optimize loop as genuine breakthrough
Self-hosting trade-off: when does 860GB VRAM requirement make GLM-5 impractical for most teams?
Benchmark credibility: SWE-bench gaming concerns with Chinese models and what decontaminated SWE-Rebench actually shows
Historic milestone: GLM-5.1 being the first open-source model to top SWE-bench Pro even if temporarily
GLM-5 vs GLM-5.1 decision guide: which version to use depending on use case, budget, and deployment needs
keywords: GLM-5 MoE Huawei Ascend open weight
